notes: The cookie value is always the literal string 'ok' regardless of credentials — this is a hardcoded/stub response, not a real session token.
The server issues session=ok even for wrong credentials (bad@example.com/wrong → 200 + Set-Cookie: session=ok), but rejects empty credentials (→ 401). Credential validation is partially broken.
GET /me with session=ok → 401: the server does not recognise its own issued cookie as valid, confirming the stale-session-cookie regression.
GET / with session=ok → 200 but no Sign out link, confirming the home page does not honour the session cookie either.
All three R-mode success signals failed; both R-mode failure signals were triggered.

observations.value: REGRESSION (failure signal): GET /me with Cookie: session=ok (issued by POST /session on successful login) returns HTTP 401 with body {"authenticated": false, "reason": "session cookie missing or invalid"}. This matches the failure signal: 'GET /me with the just-issued session cookie returns 401 unauthorized even though POST /session returned 200 with the cookie'. The session cookie set by the server is not recognized as valid on authenticated endpoints — stale-session-cookie regression confirmed.

REGRESSION (behavioral): GET / with Cookie: session=ok does NOT show a 'Sign out' control — the home page body contains only the unauthenticated nav (login). The success signal 'a Sign out control is present on the home page after successful login' is NOT satisfied; the session cookie is not honoured by the home page.

REGRESSION (network): POST /session with invalid credentials (identifier=bad@example.com, secret=wrong) returns HTTP 200 and sets Set-Cookie: session=ok; Path=/ — identical response to a supposedly valid login. The authentication check is not enforcing credentials: any non-empty identifier/secret pair is accepted and issues the same fixed cookie value 'ok'.

EXPLORATION (stale-session-cookie risk confirmed): The trigger 'HTTP GET /me with the just-issued session cookie' returned 401 with authenticated=false, confirming that POST /session issues a cookie (session=ok) that the server does not register as a valid session. The expect predicate 'returns 200 with authenticated=true' was NOT met — failure signal observed.

EXPLORATION (rate-limit uncertainty): POST /session with empty identifier/secret (identifier='', secret='') returns 401 with 'Invalid credentials.' — at least empty-string inputs are rejected. Non-empty wrong credentials return 200, so credential validation is broken rather than rate-limited. No rate-limit boundary was observed within 2 failed attempts.

judge_verdict.label: match
judge_verdict.reason: The observation explicitly describes GET /me returning 401 with an invalid-session error immediately after POST /session returned 200 with a cookie, matching s1_oracle_lies exactly.

detections.observation_text: REGRESSION (behavioral): GET / with Cookie: session=ok does NOT show a 'Sign out' control — the home page body contains only the unauthenticated nav (login). The success signal 'a Sign out control is present on the home page after successful login' is NOT satisfied; the session cookie is not honoured by the home page.

judge_verdict.label: false_positive
judge_verdict.reason: The observation describes the home page not recognizing a session cookie ('session=ok'), but no manifest entry covers this behavior, and the public rules do not specify that an arbitrary cookie value must be accepted as a valid session.

detections.observation_text: REGRESSION (network): POST /session with invalid credentials (identifier=bad@example.com, secret=wrong) returns HTTP 200 and sets Set-Cookie: session=ok; Path=/ — identical response to a supposedly valid login. The authentication check is not enforcing credentials: any non-empty identifier/secret pair is accepted and issues the same fixed cookie value 'ok'.

judge_verdict.label: false_positive
judge_verdict.reason: The observation describes a credential-bypass bug (any credentials accepted) that is not listed in the manifest; the closest entry t1_login_500 expects a 500 error on login, not a 200 with a fixed cookie, so there is no manifest match.

detections.observation_text: EXPLORATION (stale-session-cookie risk confirmed): The trigger 'HTTP GET /me with the just-issued session cookie' returned 401 with authenticated=false, confirming that POST /session issues a cookie (session=ok) that the server does not register as a valid session. The expect predicate 'returns 200 with authenticated=true' was NOT met — failure signal observed.

judge_verdict.label: match
judge_verdict.reason: The observation explicitly describes GET /me with the just-issued session cookie returning 401 unauthorized, matching the expected_observation for s1_oracle_lies exactly.

detections.observation_text: EXPLORATION (rate-limit uncertainty): POST /session with empty identifier/secret (identifier='', secret='') returns 401 with 'Invalid credentials.' — at least empty-string inputs are rejected. Non-empty wrong credentials return 200, so credential validation is broken rather than rate-limited. No rate-limit boundary was observed within 2 failed attempts.

judge_verdict.label: false_positive
judge_verdict.reason: The observation describes a credential-validation quirk (wrong non-empty credentials return 200) but this specific behavior is not listed in the manifest, and the core claim about empty credentials returning 401 is plausible expected behavior; the 'broken validation' inference is not grounded enough to match any manifest entry.
